data.title: TEAMSTERS PICKET AMAZON FACILITIES ON BOTH COASTS OVER ILLEGAL FIRINGS
data.description: Employee activists in major cities across the United States, specifically New York and Los Angeles, are actively protesting against Amazon's unethical business practices. The Amazon Teamsters at DAX7 in Los Angeles have escalated their demonstrations by expanding picket lines to the DBK4 facility due to perceived unfair labor practices. The protest, which started on April 24, 2026, is part of a larger movement to bring attention to exploitation within the company. The workers are demanding better working conditions, fair wages, and an end to mistreatment. The number of employees participating in the protest continues to grow, indicating widespread discontent among Amazon workers.

data.title: Happy Renters, Better Performance: Why Resident Experience Matters Most in Today’s Market
data.description: According to recent data, residents who are happy with their move-in experience are 31% more inclined to renew their leases. This highlights the significance of initial impressions in fostering lasting property success. This finding emphasizes the critical role of resident satisfaction in driving long-term property performance.

data.title: Hubbell Reports Regular Quarterly Dividend
data.description: Hubbell Incorporated (NYSE:HUBB) announced that its Board of Directors has approved a regular quarterly dividend of $1.42 per share on the Company’s common stock. Shareholders of record as of May 29, 2026, will receive the dividend on June 15, 2026.

data.title: Sportradar Reschedules Its First Quarter 2026 Financial and Operating Results to April 28, 2026
data.description: Sportradar Group AG, a company listed on Nasdaq under the symbol SRAD, based in St. Gallen, Switzerland, has revealed a date change for its upcoming first quarter earnings call, as disclosed on April 24, 2026.
Ipsos reported their shares and voting rights for March 2026.
Changes in financial schedule: Financial Year 2025/2026 altered.

data.title: Indivior Presents New Real-World Data at ASAM 2026 Annual Conference, Supporting Remission as a Treatment Outcome and Reinforcing the Clinical Benefits of Monthly Injectable Buprenorphine for Opioid Use Disorder
data.description: In Richmond, Virginia, on April 24, 2026, Indivior Pharmaceuticals, Inc. (Nasdaq: INDV), renowned for treating opioid use disorder, is set to unveil two scientific posters at the American Society of Addiction Medicine (ASAM) Annual Conference in San Diego, California, spanning from April 23-26, 2026. One poster is an independent presentation, while the other is a collaboration with VA Tech. The focus is on extended-release buprenorphine, available commercially as SUBLOCADE ®, and its positive impact on individuals with OUD. These findings contribute to the growing body of research into the drug's efficacy in improving outcomes for OUD patients, highlighting advancements in overdose prevention and other recovery markers.

data.title: MOORS & CABOT WELCOMES TANNER WELCH AND IAN AMBERSON TO THEIR PHOENIX, ARIZONA BRANCH
data.description: Based in Boston and established in 1890, Moors & Cabot is a well-known financial advisory firm with more than 20 branches nationwide. Recently, the firm has been focusing on enhancing the experience for financial professionals and their clients.

data.title: eMerge Americas and SmartGateVC Launch HIVE -- A Healthcare Syndicate Built to Move Industry Capital into Vetted Innovation
data.description: A new initiative called the Healthcare Innovation Venture Ecosystem (HIVE) has brought together top executives from major healthcare institutions in the United States. Their aim is to collectively invest in and support promising HealthTech startups, expediting their growth with efficiency and alignment. This collaboration was announced in Miami on April 24, 2026, during the eMerge Americas event.

data.title: The American Waterways Operators Issues Statement on Jones Act Waiver Extension
data.description: In Arlington, Virginia on April 24, 2026, the American Waterways Operators, the leading trade association representing the American tugboat, towboat, and barge sector, issued a statement regarding the Trump Administration's plan to prolong the expansive Jones Act. The Act requires goods transported between U.S. ports to be carried exclusively by vessels built in the United States, owned by U.S. citizens, and crewed by U.S. citizens or permanent residents. The extension is expected to impact various aspects of the industry, with estimates suggesting it will potentially influence trade volumes and shipping costs, affecting over 40,000 vessels and around 500,000 jobs associated with the sector.

data.title: NEW STUDY REVEALS BIG COMPOSITIONAL SHIFT IN SMALL NEAR-EARTH ASTEROIDS
data.description: A recent study conducted by an international team in FLAGSTAFF, Arizona has revealed a significant discovery regarding the compositions of near-Earth asteroids (NEAs). The analysis of 189 NEAs has showcased a distinct trend that is dependent on their sizes. This finding is expected to enhance our understanding of meteorite origins and improve impact-risk models. The study, conducted in April 2026, provides valuable insights that could potentially influence future research and discussions on the subject.

data.title: Delivery Hero Unveils Herogen – Autonomous AI Agent Unlocks 130-Person Engineering Output
data.description: In Berlin, the prominent local delivery platform, Delivery Hero, has introduced Herogen - a cutting-edge autonomous software delivery agent to its employees. Herogen was initially presented to the company's product and engineering teams in February. Developed internally using top-of-the-line Large Language Models (LLMs), Herogen currently generates an annual coding volume equivalent to that of 130 senior engineers. Moreover, its capabilities are swiftly increasing. To ensure autonomous security and quality, the platform has implemented the "Council of Agents."
Bidders to select clients for SoftBank US data center, Google and Microsoft to be among bidders - Nikkei

data.title: Hindustan Zinc clocks record Q4 net profit of US$ 550 million, up 68% YoY; EBITDA at US$ 847 million, driven by efficiency-led growth
data.description: Hindustan Zinc Limited, a Vedanta Group company and the world's largest integrated zinc producer based in Udaipur, India, disclosed its financial performance for the fourth quarter and fiscal year ending on March 31, 2026. The company achieved unprecedented results, with a record revenue of US$ 4,623 million for the year, marking a 20% increase year-over-year (YoY). Additionally, they reported an all-time high EBITDA of US$ 2,508 million, reflecting a 27% YoY growth, and showcased an exceptional EBITDA margin of 54%. Notably, the net profit for the year reached a record US$ 1,565 million, demonstrating a 34% YoY surge. In the fourth quarter, EBITDA hit a record US$ 847 million, rising by 61% YoY, while net profit soared to a record US$ 550 million, up by 68% from the previous year. For more details, the complete article is accessible on Benzinga.com.

data.title: RFK Jr. aide linked to wellness firm profiting from health savings accounts, while Trump pushed policies; Calley Means still led the company in 2021.
data.description: During the previous year, Calley Means continued to serve as the president of a company that heavily depended on health savings accounts. This occurred alongside the Trump administration's efforts to enhance these accounts through policy developments.
